title: Need help with custom Automation
excerpt: I am fairly new to Monday and trying to create an automation using the following logic:  If a new item has not been created in the last 7 days, then an email notification will be sent to an individual.  Any help would be…

posts.author: OscarTerry (Oscar Terry)
posts.cookedHtml: I am fairly new to Monday and trying to create an automation using the following logic: If a new item has not been created in the last 7 days, then an email notification will be sent to an individual. Any help would be appreciated.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @OscarTerry

monday automations can’t directly trigger based on something not happening, like no new item being created. But you can get very close with a simple workaround.

Here’s a common approach that works well:

  1. Add a Date column like “Last item created”

  2. Create an automation:
    When an item is created → set “Last item created” to today

  3. Create a scheduled automation:
    Every day → if “Last item created” is more than 7 days ago → notify or email a person

This way, you’re tracking the last creation date and letting a scheduled automation do the check for you.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Tuesday Wizard

posts.author: OscarTerry (Oscar Terry)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the help. I am struggling with how to create the “ is more than 7 days ago” logic. I don’t see it within the automation options.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
monday is still limited on the ‘conditional ‘ logic you can add to an automation/workflow that starts with ‘every time period’. Can you maybe give a little more about your current set up/goals? Maybe we can help think of another option?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: OscarTerry (Oscar Terry)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a tracking log board for newly created items that are expected a certain number of new entries over a 7 day period ( a quota of sorts ). I was hoping Monday could notify me, as the manager, if a new entry has NOT been created over a 7 day period, notifying me that they are not doing the required entries of 1 every 7 days.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
OscarTerry:

I am fairly new to Monday and trying to create an automation using the following logic: If a new item has not been created in the last 7 days, then an email notification will be sent to an individual. Any help would be appreciated.

Hi @OscarTerry You can do this with Make.com even if you’re new to Monday. The flow would look like this:

  1. Every day, Make checks your board for items created in the last 7 days.

  2. If it doesn’t find any, it automatically sends an email notification to the person you specify.

This way, you don’t have to manually track the board—Make handles the checking and emailing for you.
